task: Estimate the prevalence and identify risk factors of undiagnosed obstructive lung disease among U.S. adults using NHANES 2007-2012 data. The analysis should: (1) define obstructive lung disease using pre-bronchodilator spirometry criteria (FEV1/FVC < lower limit of normal [LLN] based on NHANES III reference equations), and classify participants as 'diagnosed' (self-reported physician diagnosis of COPD, emphysema, or chronic bronchitis) or 'undiagnosed' (spirometric obstruction present but no self-reported diagnosis); (2) estimate the population-weighted prevalence of undiagnosed obstructive lung disease overall and stratified by age group, sex, race/ethnicity, smoking status, and disease severity (GOLD stages); (3) identify factors independently associated with being undiagnosed among those with spirometric obstruction using survey-weighted multivariable logistic regression, adjusting for demographics, smoking history, comorbidity burden, healthcare utilization, self-rated health, respiratory symptoms, and lung function severity; (4) link NHANES III participants with the National Death Index to evaluate long-term mortality risk of undiagnosed versus diagnosed obstructive lung disease compared to those without obstruction, using Cox proportional hazards models. The study population includes adults aged 20-79 years with acceptable spirometry quality grades. All analyses must account for the complex survey design of NHANES (MEC examination weights, PSU, strata). NHANES 2007-2012 data (3 cycles) should be combined using appropriate multi-cycle weighting procedures.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2012 Demographics (3 cycles)
data.description: Demographic variables from 2007-2008 (DEMO_E), 2009-2010 (DEMO_F), and 2011-2012 (DEMO_G) cycles. Includes age, sex, race/ethnicity, education, income, and country of birth.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2012 Spirometry (3 cycles)
data.description: Pre- and post-bronchodilator spirometry from SPX_E, SPX_F, and SPX_G. Includes FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, quality grades, and predicted values based on NHANES III reference equations.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2012 Medical Conditions Questionnaire (3 cycles)
data.description: Self-reported physician diagnoses from MCQ_E/F/G including ever told you have COPD, emphysema, or chronic bronchitis. Also includes self-reported asthma, cancer, heart disease, and diabetes for comorbidity assessment.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2012 Respiratory Health and Smoking (3 cycles)
data.description: Respiratory symptoms questionnaire and smoking history from SMQ_E/F/G. Includes cough, phlegm, wheezing, shortness of breath, pack-years of smoking, and current smoking status.

data.name: NHANES 2007-2012 Healthcare Utilization and Self-Rated Health
data.description: Number of healthcare visits, insurance status, and self-rated general health from HUQ_E/F/G. Used to assess healthcare access and health perception in relation to diagnostic status.

data.name: Variable Codebook
data.description: Complete variable definitions, value labels, and survey design notes for all NHANES 2007-2012 components used in this analysis.
